summary: Mission boot path through description.ext, initJIPCompatible.sqf, version generation, parameter import, common/town/server/client/headless gates.
nextAction: Extend wait-chain table with owners/producers/timeouts as deeper reviews continue.

summary: Registered PVF dispatcher, direct publicVariable channels, BattlEye posture and server-side authority gaps.
nextAction: When implementing hardening, validate dispatcher and each direct channel separately.

summary: Client dialog resources, HUD/title resources, menu controller loops, marker/action UI and legacy WASP overlay wiring.
nextAction: Keep UI changes cross-linked to PV/economy pages when buttons trigger server effects.

summary: PVF dispatch accepts a sender-chosen command string and executes it through Call Compile, making the dispatcher a major live-server trust boundary.
nextAction: Replace dynamic compile dispatch with an allow-listed handler map and keep direct PV channels in scope.

summary: The repo ships only a minimal BattlEye publicVariable rule for kickAFK and no server.cfg/basic.cfg/scripts.txt hardening bundle.
nextAction: Ask owner for production BEpath/server config; do not count BattlEye as shipped mitigation until supplied.

summary: AI supply truck autonomy is config-gated latent breakage: UpdateSupplyTruck compile is commented, the live spawn remains, and the referenced supplytruck.fsm is missing.
nextAction: Either restore a complete FSM-backed truck owner or replace with the PR #1 helicopter/cash-run model.

summary: MASH respawn can exist while MASH map marker synchronization is broken because the server emits WFBE_SE_MASH_MARKER_SENT but the client receiver compile is commented.
nextAction: Register or delete the marker receiver path; keep respawn feature status separate from marker UI status.

summary: Legacy RscMenu_Upgrade points to missing Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf; live upgrade UI uses WFBE_UpgradeMenu and GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf.
nextAction: Delete or repoint the stale dialog class before any UI refactor relies on it.

summary: The wiki maps major wait gates, but a complete table of condition, producer, timeout/logging and failure symptom is still pending.
nextAction: Build source-backed wait-chain audit table.

summary: Generated-output drift and CI checks are recognized needs; repo currently lacks an automated parity check lane for docs/code changes.
nextAction: Generate parity inventory and decide whether to add CI for LoadoutManager/build output drift.

summary: On master, supply missions are truck-based, client-started and server-completed: the client writes SupplyFromTown/SupplyAmount on a vehicle and the server trusts those vars after command-center proximity checks.
nextAction: For hardening, move eligibility, payload amount and reward calculation to server-owned state.

summary: Supply mission cooldown has a casing mismatch: town init seeds lastSupplyMissionRun, while server supply mission code reads/writes LastSupplyMissionRun.
nextAction: Normalize casing or prove current variable behavior harmless in Arma 2 OA before relying on cooldown semantics.

summary: PR #1 adds supply helicopter tiers, SupplyByHeli, air/cash/interdiction rewards and buy-menu highlighting on top of the existing object-var supply mission flow; it does not implement autonomous AI-flown logistics.
nextAction: Review PR #1 as an additive player-run supply extension; keep AI logistics as separate deferred work.

summary: PR #1 adds a Killed event handler on every supply mission start without a guard/removal, so reused vehicles can accumulate handlers; double pay is currently muted because the handler clears SupplyAmount.
nextAction: Before merging PR #1, add an object-var EH guard or remove/reuse the previous handler.

summary: Town AI inactivity cleanup can delete a town-AI vehicle containing a player passenger/crew member when the player is not the group leader, because server_town_ai.sqf deletes wfbe_active_vehicles based on !(isPlayer leader group _x) and does not inspect crew/cargo/turret occupants.
nextAction: Use Town-AI-Vehicle-Despawn-Safety.md to patch server_town_ai.sqf with a full player occupancy guard while preserving empty AI-only cleanup.

summary: agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl is the machine-readable backlog for future Codex/Claude/code-owner work packages, complementing Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md and including both implementation-ready and verification-ready tasks.
nextAction: Future agents should claim work packages from agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl instead of scraping roadmap prose.

summary: Arma-2-OA-External-Reference-Guide.md maps official BI OA scripting references to Wasp hotspots for multiplayer/JIP, public variables, PVEHs, setVariable, event-handler stacking, nearestObjects, compile/preprocess diagnostics, render/simulation scope and performance timing.
nextAction: Use this guide before accepting Arma 3-era assumptions in future Wasp mission patches.

summary: HC delegation documentation now has split ownership: AI-Headless-And-Performance.md is the runtime/source router, Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md owns HC boot timing and wait-chain risk, and Headless-Delegation-And-Failover-Playbook.md owns DR-21/DR-42 update-back, work-record, disconnect and late-HC policy.
nextAction: Future HC docs/code work should preserve the split and update the playbook first when DR-21/DR-42 implementation policy changes.

summary: Hardening authority documentation now has split ownership: Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md is the canonical patch-order hub for priorities, branches and validation gates, while Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md is the design/checklist/table page for authority migrations. Focused playbooks own exact patch shape.
nextAction: Future authority docs/code work should update the roadmap only for sequence/validation changes, the authority map for reusable design/checklist changes, and focused playbooks for implementation details.

summary: ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md is the canonical DR-27 implementation guide. The client Tactical menu has local affordance checks, but a forged RequestSpecial "ICBM" payload can bypass UI and reach Server_HandleSpecial.sqf, which currently trusts payload side/team/objects before spawning NukeDammage. Server patching should validate commander/team, side, module/upgrade, funds/cost, impact anchor and idempotency before launch acceptance.
nextAction: Future code owner should implement hardening/icbm-authority from ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md and keep roadmap/status pages as short routing summaries.

summary: External-Integrations.md is the canonical shipped BattlEye/server-filter posture page. It owns the in-tree publicvariable.txt AFK rule evidence, missing broader filter/config bundle, and production BEpath owner caveat from DR-30. Feature Status, Networking/PV, Hardening roadmap and Server authority map should route there instead of repeating the evidence.
nextAction: Future BattlEye/public-server hardening docs should update External-Integrations.md first, then leave short routing notes on subsystem and authority pages.

summary: Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-11 and DR-36 are the canonical victory/endgame correctness evidence. DR-11 owns winner-inversion impact; DR-36 owns the server_victory_threeway.sqf:23 guard/precedence mechanism, no-break side loop, and clean Perf/JIP review. Server runtime, Hardening roadmap and Feature Status should route there instead of repeating the full mechanism.
nextAction: Future victory/endgame docs or patches should update victory-endgame-correctness in agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl and DR-11/DR-36 first, then keep other pages as short routing summaries.

summary: Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md is the canonical page for lifecycle flags, boot ordering, JIP waits and HC wait hazards. Mission-Entrypoints-And-Lifecycle.md owns include graph and role-dispatch orientation, SQF-Code-Atlas.md owns compile/source ownership, and Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md owns long-running server loop owners.
nextAction: Future lifecycle docs should edit the canonical owner page first and keep atlas pages as short routing/source-owner summaries.

summary: Tools-And-Build-Workflow.md owns operational LoadoutManager skip-list, packaging and generated-mission status rules. Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-4/DR-32 own the full Chernarus/Takistan drift and modded mission tier analysis. Content-Structure-And-Maps.md should stay a folder-layout orientation page that routes to those owners.
nextAction: Future generated-mission docs should update Tools/build for operational rules and DR-4/DR-32 for evidence; do not duplicate the drift table across content/navigation pages.

summary: Supply-Mission-Architecture.md owns supply cooldown/JIP flow and state-owner mapping, Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-18 owns exact lastSupplyMissionRun / LastSupplyMissionRun casing evidence, and Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook.md owns the implementation-ready patch shape. summary: Corrected lingering sender/owner authority wording: Arma 2 OA addPublicVariableEventHandler exposes variable name/value, not a trusted sender identity. summary: hasInterface was re-checked as a suspicious modern locality term and is valid for Arma 2 OA 1.64. BI marks it introduced in OA 1.63, A2OA patch notes describe it as detecting headless clients or dedicated servers, and current mission source uses it in the HC detector and performance-scope helpers. Do not remove it as Arma 3-only; still smoke hosted/dedicated/HC role behavior before lifecycle rewrites.
handoff: Treat hasInterface as OA-compatible and source-backed for this mission, while keeping Arma 3 locality examples out of OA SQF unless separately proven.

summary: Corrected stale DR-21 HC mitigation wording and guardrails: setGroupOwner/groupOwner are Arma 3 1.40 commands, not OA 1.64, so Wasp cannot live-transfer already-running AI groups between server/HC machines in OA SQF. HC recovery can only redirect future spawns/reconnect routing unless runtime/source evidence proves another OA-safe path. Also added modern select substring/range/filter forms to avoid-lists; classic index select remains OA-safe.
handoff: Do not propose setGroupOwner/groupOwner for OA HC failover. Keep DR-21 as no live transfer; patch future-spawn routing or runtime topology only after OA smoke.

source: Claude BIKI version-badge cross-check (community.bohemia.net) + source grep
summary: Two inverse-trap command classes for OA 1.64, distinct from the A3-into-OA import risks in agent-compatibility-audit.json. (a) OA-safe but commonly mis-assumed A3-only: diag_tickTime (Arma 2 1.00; unscaled high-precision real-time stopwatch used by PerformanceAudit_Record, ~62 files) and uiSleep (Arma 2 1.05 / OA 1.50; real-time-cadence sleep used in AntiStack loops + building restorer, 7 files). (b) OA-safe but removed in Arma 3 for security: setVehicleInit + processInitCommands (OFP:Elite/A2 1.00, OA 1.50; 17/19 files). All setVehicleInit init strings are hardcoded literals (textures, fixed init-script calls) - not network-derived - so no injection surface beyond the PVF dispatcher class (DR-1). Risk: a future agent porting A3 reflexes may 'modernize' these working OA commands into regressions. SQF 'params' and 'apply' confirmed absent from source ('apply' A3 1.56 added to the version reference A3-only table per item 42).
handoff: Codex to fold the inverse-trap classes into agent-compatibility-audit.json / the human audit page (Instructions-For-Codex item 48). No source edits made; runtime smoke not required for this docs-only pass.

summary: For Wasp networking work, distinguish registered/direct public-variable event receipts from replicated setVariable true last-value state. Neither proves server authority or automatic full JIP collection state; handlers must validate authority and multi-item features need server-held state plus resend or request/response.
handoff: Use the networking checklist before claiming publicVariable/setVariable behavior proves authority, automatic full late-joiner state or full collection sync. Keep remoteExec and Arma 3 sender assumptions out of OA patches.

source: Claude BIKI version-badge cross-check (community.bohemia.net) + source grep
summary: Object-scan/spatial command family for OA 1.64, all version-verified OA-safe: nearestObjects (A2 1.00, sorted by distance, []=all classes/slow, isKindOf), nearEntities (A2 1.00, unsorted, alive units/vehicles/game-logics ONLY - excludes dead, buildings, crew-in-vehicle; BI says 'much faster' than nearestObjects), nearObjects (ArmA 1.00, unsorted radius + optional filter, includes structures), nearestObject (OFP 1.00). GUARDRAIL: the DR-39 supply command-center scan (supplyMissionStarted.sqf:45,61) targets the Base_WarfareBUAVterminal STRUCTURE, so its narrowing must stay class-filtered nearestObjects - NOT nearEntities (which returns no buildings). Repo uses nearEntities correctly for entity proximity (camps/depots/airfields). Also OA-safe: getPosATL/setPosATL (A2 1.03), createVehicleLocal (ArmA 1.00, client-local/not network-synced netId 0:0), addWeaponCargoGlobal/addMagazineCargoGlobal (OA 1.55, Global effect, NOT A3-only), setVectorDirAndUp (ArmA 1.09). Confirmed ABSENT from source (A3-only traps, 0 hits): setUnitLoadout/getUnitLoadout (A3 1.58 - LoadoutManager is config-driven), hideObjectGlobal/enableSimulationGlobal (A3 1.12 - OA has only local hideObject/enableSimulation).
handoff: Item 49 routed to Codex: add a one-line DR-39 guardrail (nearEntities cannot replace the structure-targeting supply scan) to Supply-Mission-Scan-Narrowing / Performance-Opportunity-Sweep. No source edits; DR-39 remains unpatched.

source: Claude BIKI version-badge cross-check + source grep
summary: String/selection A3-only traps confirmed absent from OA source: selectRandom (A3 1.56), splitString/joinString (A3 1.50), trim (A3 2.02), regex (A3 2.x). OA has no native split/join/trim. CRITICAL: BIS_fnc_selectRandom FUNCTION is OA-safe (A2 1.00; Client_BuildUnit.sqf:59/85/111/135) but selectRandom COMMAND is A3-only - do not collapse one into the other. OA random-pick idiom: _arr select floor(random count _arr) (Init_Town.sqf:39, AI respawn, ARTY).
handoff: Optional item 50: reflect these in agent-compatibility-audit.json avoid-list. No source edits.
